completed_slice: Recovered and committed the semantic precedence model required to reconcile a stale canonical queue with later append-only continuity updates without erasing provenance.
resolved: The effective-state interpretation now has an explicit semantic precedence model rather than relying on timestamp recency alone.
Canonical governance, append-only observations, and explicit reconciliation are preserved as distinct source classes.
remaining: Implement an executable effective-queue reconciler using the committed precedence model.
Execute an authenticated exhaustive branch and per-branch commit-history collector.
Produce an independent eligible-path set for operations/queue-updates/*.json.
Locate or exhaustively classify the originating sources for M-004, M-005, and M-006.
privacy_boundary: Public repository governance files and commit metadata only; no private chat, health, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploads included.
next_action: Implement a fail-closed reconciler that consumes the canonical baseline plus an independently enumerated queue-update set, emits source-complete effective state, and rejects omitted, duplicated, unbound, or semantically ambiguous updates.
